differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Updates only changed DOM nodes, no Virtual DOM.
differences.rightDesc: Avoids re-executing existing JS, loads minimal code.

differences.title: 2. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Signals, effects, and mappers for efficient updates.
differences.rightDesc: State serialized and resumed on interaction.

differences.title: 3. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Compiles JSX to efficient vanilla JS and DOM nodes.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles JSX with lazy loading and resumability in mind.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Extremely fast updates due to no Virtual DOM.
differences.rightDesc: Near-instant initial loads, minimal JS parse time.

differences.title: 5. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Similar to React's JSX, easier transition for React devs.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding of resumability and lazy loading concepts.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Solid community, libraries, and tooling are developing.
differences.rightDesc: Newer, actively growing, with focus on QwikCity framework.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for interactive UIs needing maximum runtime speed.
differences.rightDesc: Great for blogs, e-commerce, landing pages needing fast initial load.
